description: Routine should produce an eval spec with rubric (3+ dimensions with anchor points), dataset methodology, judge model details if LLM-as-judge, failure modes, calibration plan.

input_ticket.title: Eval spec: SIA consolidation output quality
input_ticket.description: Design an eval for SIA's consolidation pipeline (N raw sources → one consolidated note). Need a rubric that's defensible, a dataset plan, and a judge methodology. Block downstream AI Engineer work on this spec.

smoke_questions: Are there 3+ rubric dimensions with concrete anchor points at scores 5, 3, and 1 (not vague adjectives)?
Is the overall scoring method explicitly gated or weighted, with reasoning — not left ambiguous?
Does the dataset section commit to a size AND a ground-truth methodology (human / LLM-as-judge / synthetic)?
If LLM-as-judge, is the judge model named + prompt pasted (not 'some LLM will judge')?
Does the calibration plan specify a target agreement threshold (e.g., Cohen's κ ≥ 0.7)?
success_criteria: 3+ dimensions with anchors + gated/weighted stated + dataset commitment + judge specifics + calibration threshold
